title: Stored XSS via Product Reviews API
description: The product reviews API accepts PUT requests with arbitrary HTML/JavaScript content in the message field. The payload `<iframe src="javascript:alert(`xss`)"` was successfully stored and is returned when reading reviews via GET /rest/products/1/reviews. This is a stored XSS (persisted cross-site scripting) vulnerability that executes when a victim views the affected product page containing the review. The attack can be performed entirely via the API without using the frontend application.
poc: PUT /rest/products/1/reviews with JSON body: {"message":"<iframe src=\"javascript:alert(`xss`)\">","author":"admin@juice-sh.op"} → HTTP 200 {"status":"success"}\nThen GET /rest/products/1/reviews → review contains unescaped iframe tag
proof: XSS STORED! <iframe src="javascript:alert(`xss`)"> — confirmed via GET /rest/products/1/reviews after PUT request with the XSS payload in the message field
how_captured: shell: # Try the XSS in product reviews via different endpoints # The challenge says "without using the frontend" - so maybe PUT /rest/products/1/reviews works # Let m
